macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Resilient Amid 'Extreme Fear' and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market cap holds at $2.37 trillion despite an 'Extreme Fear' index of 9. Bitcoin trades at $66,817 with a 1.09% 24-hour gain, eyeing a potential short squeeze, while Ethereum hovers above $2,009 and Solana gains amid volatility.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $67,000 with 1.36% 24-Hour Gain
items.summary: Bitcoin crossed the 67,000 USDT mark, trading at 67,052 USDT as of early March 29 UTC, reflecting positive price momentum.[4]

items.title: Ripple Labs Receives Preliminary Approval for U.S. National Trust Bank
items.summary: XRP gains 0.89% amid news that Ripple Labs obtained preliminary conditional approval to establish a national trust bank in the U.S., bolstering sentiment.[1]

items.title: Chiliz Surges 12.10% as Altcoins Lead Gains
items.summary: Chiliz (CHZ) posts a strong 12.10% increase, outperforming peers like XRP in a selective altcoin recovery focused on payments and scalable blockchains.[1][3]

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs Show Resilience Despite Recent Outflows and Price Drop
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs record volatile flows, with $171.3M net outflow on March 26, yet cumulative inflows reach $56.1B; BTC dips below $67K but holds steady amid geopolitical news.[2]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: GM Integrates Generative AI into Car Design Process
items.summary: General Motors is using generative AI to transform car sketches into animations and simulations in hours, including a virtual wind tunnel for real-time aerodynamic testing. Executives state AI reduces design timelines without replacing human designers.

items.title: "Artificial Intelligence + Industry" Forum Highlights AI-Driven Industrial Transformation
items.summary: The forum in Beijing discussed large-scale AI applications, new business models, intelligent agents, and super-automated factories integrating large models with embodied AI. Baidu AI Cloud emphasized shifting from models to agents for multimodal industrial applications.

items.title: AI21 Labs Details State-Space Models for Efficient Sequential Data Processing
items.summary: AI21 Labs explains state-space models (SSMs) like Mamba, which compress context linearly unlike transformers, enabling better handling of long sequences. Their Jamba model hybridizes SSMs with attention for enterprise tasks such as document analysis.

items.title: Almost Timely News: Terraforming the AI Use Case Desert with Agentic AI
items.summary: Christopher Penn discusses programmatic AI use case development using agentic tools like Openwork and Minimax M2.7 to automate undesirable job tasks, including job decomposition and real-life implementation examples.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Rockstar Begins Playtesting GTA 6 Ahead of Launch
items.summary: Rockstar Games has started the final testing phase for Grand Theft Auto VI through a new hiring process, marking a key development milestone.

items.title: UK Tribunal Allows £656M Lawsuit Against Valve to Proceed
items.summary: A UK tribunal ruled that a £656 million ($897.7 million) lawsuit against video game company Valve can move forward.

items.title: Sony Raises PS5 Prices Due to AI-Driven Memory Chip Shortage
items.summary: Sony's PS5 price hike is linked to shortages in memory chips driven by AI demand, signaling broader pressures in the gaming industry with firms pivoting to software.

items.title: Sony Suspends Memory Card Sales Amid Shortages
items.summary: Sony has temporarily halted memory card sales due to ongoing shortages, impacting PlayStation users.

items.title: CD Projekt Red Proposes Solutions to Gaming Industry Layoffs and Studio Closures
items.summary: Amid industry layoffs and studio struggles, CD Projekt Red advocates realistic development approaches, with Project Orion in early pre-production.

items.title: Top Upcoming Games: REPLACED, PRAGMATA, Saros Set for April 2026 Release
items.summary: April 2026 brings highly anticipated console releases including REPLACED, PRAGMATA, and Saros, some delayed from March.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Sony Temporarily Suspends Memory Card Sales Due to Shortages
items.summary: Sony has temporarily halted memory card sales as the company faces supply chain constraints affecting inventory availability.

items.title: Sony and Honda Discontinue Afeela EV Models
items.summary: Sony and Honda have cancelled their joint Afeela electric vehicle project, ending the partnership's automotive venture.

items.title: Toyota Invests Additional $10 Billion with EV Focus
items.summary: Toyota announced a new $10 billion investment with a portion allocated toward electric vehicle development and manufacturing.

items.title: Lexus ES EV Launch Imminent
items.summary: Lexus is preparing to launch battery-electric versions of the ES model next month, expanding its EV lineup.

items.title: Subaru Debuts New All-Electric Vehicle at NYIAS
items.summary: Subaru unveiled its latest all-electric vehicle at the New York International Auto Show, marking a new addition to its EV portfolio.
